name: Gawri

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Gawri (kala1373).

notes: Skeleton. Gawri (Kalam Kohistani), a Dardic Indo-Aryan language of the Swat and Dir Kohistan valleys of Pakistan, written in an Urdu-based Arabic script. Urdu letter values apply: retroflex ٹ ڈ ڑ, postalveolars چ ج ش ژ, and aspiration/breathiness written with do-chashmi he ھ (mapped both as digraphs and as a bare aspiration mark); the Perso-Arabic loan letters ث ذ ص ض ط ظ fold into s/z/t. Short vowels are written only defectively and vowel length, nasalization and the language's pitch accents are largely unrecoverable, so a high PER floor is expected. Any Gawri-specific extra letters beyond the Urdu set (e.g. for retroflex fricatives) are not mapped.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Gawri (kala1373)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
